message:
test(quake): port pre-existing clip_polygon_near tests to clip_triangle_near

Follow-up to 798e5bb0 (perf: replace clip_polygon_near Vec with
stack ClippedTriangle). The previous commit added three new tests
for the new function but missed updating the four pre-existing
tests that still referenced the old `clip_polygon_near` signature
(`&[Vec3]` / `Vec<Vec3>`). They would have been pruned by
`cargo build` as dead-name references on the next CI run.

Update each:

- clip_empty_polygon  → clipped_triangle_starts_empty
  The old test exercised "empty input → empty output" against the
  Vec form; with the new fixed-size buffer that's a property of
  `ClippedTriangle::new()`, so assert that directly instead of
  feeding an empty triangle (which is no longer expressible at
  the type level).

- clip_all_in_front, clip_all_behind, clip_partial_produces_valid
  _polygon
  Same logic, signature-converted: `let poly = [...]` → `let tri =
  [...]`, `clip_polygon_near(&poly, near)` → `clip_triangle_near
  (tri, near)`, and `for v in &result` → `for v in
  result.as_slice()`. `result.is_empty()` becomes
  `result.as_slice().is_empty()` since `ClippedTriangle` does not
  Deref to slice.

Also tighten the new `clip_triangle_near_keeps_fully_visible_
triangle` from 798e5bb0: the previous `assert_eq!(clipped.as_slice(),
tri.as_slice())` exact-equality check is fragile across f32
operations. Switch to len-then-elementwise EPS comparison, matching
the style of the other geometry tests in this file.

Section divider comment renamed from "clip_polygon_near tests" to
"clip_triangle_near tests" to match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
